title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
A woman reveals that a recent codicil to a will requires Diana to marry Mr. Barrington to inherit her estate; otherwise, the property goes to her cousin, Harry Dellhouse. Hercule Poirot uses this information to debunk a theory that Diana murdered her stepfather to secure her inheritance. He explains that the crime scene was staged to look like a suicide, but the killer made several errors.

Poirot identifies the secretary, Jeffrey King (also referred to as Jeffrey Keen), as the murderer. Poirot explains that King killed the victim earlier using a silencer, then staged the body to appear as a suicide. To create a false timeline, King fired a military pistol from a living room window at 8:12 PM to mimic the sound of a gunshot. However, King failed to notice that Diana had been in the garden picking flowers, which left footprints in the flower bed. The killer had stepped on the flower bed to hide his own tracks, but the presence of only four footprints proved someone had wiped them away between Diana's visits to the garden. Furthermore, King attempted to hide a bullet he had picked up by claiming it was a silk rosebud. Upon Poirot's accusation, King collapses.

Following the resolution, Diana expresses a desire to marry Captain Marshall regardless of the inheritance, and Harry offers to split the estate with her. In a separate scene, Poirot receives a desperate, mysterious phone call from a woman at "Swan Garden" claiming she is in danger and mentioning a table with yellow irises. Upon arriving at Swan Garden, Poirot notes that only one table features yellow irises, while the others have red tulips.
